The Constitution was never printed; it was handwritten by Prem Behari Narain Raizada in flowing Italic style.
The original copies are kept in special helium-filled cases in the Library of the Parliament.
It is the longest written constitution of any sovereign country in the world.
Each page was decorated by artists from Shantiniketan, including Beohar Rammanohar Sinha.
The original draft was modified over 2,000 times before final adoption.
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee.
The Constituent Assembly took 2 years, 11 months, and 18 days to complete the draft.
The Constitution was adopted on Nov 26, 1949, but came into force on Jan 26, 1950.
The words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' were added to the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment in 1976.
The Indian Constitution is known as a 'Bag of Borrowings' for adopting best features from many countries.
The concept of 'Five Year Plans' was borrowed from the USSR.
The Right to Education (Art 21-A) became a Fundamental Right only in 2002.
The 61st Amendment lowered the voting age from 21 to 18 in 1989.
India has the largest democratic electorate in the world.
The Supreme Court of India held its first sitting on January 28, 1950.
The national emblem was adopted on the same day the Constitution came into effect.
Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first to sign the final document.
The Constitution has 12 Schedules and 448+ Articles as of today.
The 'Order of Precedence' in India is defined broadly by constitutional roles.
The President of India is the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces.
